A.missing: no agent DOING it (act); order muddled — DO clause has no time link, TOUCH used for association
A.reason: Skeleton of separation via TOUCH but the agentive act of removing is not tied to the state change.

B.missing: no agent/DO — reads as a mere happening of two things ceasing to be near, not an act of removing from association
B.reason: State-change of NEAR but no one does it, so genus 'act' is dropped.

C.reason: Someone does something so that a thing is no longer near another — agentive act causing removal from association, criterial work present.

D.reason: Someone acts on a thing, and because of that it is no longer near another it was near — act of removing from association rendered sharply.

E.missing: association rendered as DO-with (joint action) rather than being together/near; muddled roles
E.reason: Uses comitative DO for association and the causal chain is tangled, weakening the removal-from-association reading.

F.missing: association fixed as being located among PEOPLE, over-specific and drops the generic near-relation; no causal link
F.reason: Renders a thing removed from a group of people, an odd narrowing that doesn't cover the general sense.

G.missing: no explicit BECAUSE tying the act to the result
G.reason: Agent does something, before it a thing was near another, after it is not — act of removal captured though causation only implied by sequence.
